data.description:
## Why

The reconciler concentrates the codebase's accidental complexity. Three functions
carry irreducible domain logic *plus* avoidable structure (deep nesting,
stringly-typed field ladders, mutable accumulator out-params). Reducing the
*accidental* part improves editability and lowers the chance of a sync bug, while
leaving the *essential* orchestration spine intact. (Validator confirmed the
essential-vs-accidental split: `reconcile_once`'s fetchâ†’diffâ†’apply spine is
essential narrative and must NOT be flattened.)

## What

Three targeted, behavior-preserving refactors: (1) flatten
`differ.compute_mutations`'s guard cascade (the `_emit` helper is *already*
centralized â€” this is about nesting, not de-dup); (2) convert
`_apply_inbound_update`'s simple-field ladder to a declarative field-map table
(keeping the `status` AND `labels` branches special-cased); (3) replace
`create_one`'s mutable accumulator out-params with a `DispatchContext` dataclass
(this does **not** apply to `update_one` â€” see correction below).

## Scope

- In scope: `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/differ.py`, `apply_inbound.py`,
  `batch_dispatch.py`.
- Out of scope: `reconcile_once` (`reconcile.py:550`, F(82)) â€” its complexity is
  essential orchestration; do not flatten the spine. (Its inline corrupt-snapshot
  handler and OMâ†’Mutation conversion *could* be extracted later, but that is a
  separate, optional ticket â€” not this one.)

## Context (citations)

- **`differ.compute_mutations` â€” F(61), deep guard cascade:**
  `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/differ.py:177`. The 4-way local-vs-Jira
  semantic is essential, but the first arm is a deep guard cascade
  (duplicate_local_id â†’ bound â†’ ambiguous-binding-probe â†’ normal create).
  **PLAN-REVIEW CORRECTION:** emission is **already centralized** through one
  `_emit(...)` helper (def at `differ.py:134`, 7 call sites at
  288/332/355/432/457/501/536) â€” it is NOT a copy-pasted 15-line block. So the win
  here is **flattening the nesting**, not de-duplicating `_emit`. Do not "extract a
  shared `_emit`" â€” it already exists.
- **`_apply_inbound_update` â€” F(52), stringly-typed ladder:**
  `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_inbound.py:260`. A long
  `if "title" / elif "summary" / if "description" / if "priority" / if "assignee" /
  if "ticket_type" / if "parent_id" / if "status" / if "labels" â€¦` field-mapping
  ladder (simple fields â‰ˆ292-313). **Two branches will NOT collapse into the
  table and must stay special-cased:** `status` (â‰ˆ320-346: writes a *second* STATUS
  event + reverse-maps a dict shape) and **`labels`** (â‰ˆ348-360+: read-modify-write
  of the tag set). The field-map table covers only the simple scalar fields.
- **`create_one` â€” E(40), accumulator out-params + magic budget:**
  `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/batch_dispatch.py:83`. Takes
  `deferred_creates: list`, `events_list: list`, `comment_errors: list[str]` and
  appends to them as side effects (8-param signature); bare magic `200` REST budget
  at `:95` and `:125`. **PLAN-REVIEW CORRECTION:** `update_one` (`:307`) is
  `update_one(mutation, client, comment_errors=None)` â€” only **3 params, NO
  `deferred_creates`/`events_list`, NO budget**. So `DispatchContext` applies to
  `create_one` only; `update_one`'s signature is already small â€” leave it (it may
  take the `ctx` for `comment_errors` for consistency, but that is optional).

## Implementation plan

1. **`compute_mutations`:** extract each guard arm of the unbound-local cascade
   into a small helper that returns early (e.g.
   `_emit_for_unbound_local(key, fields, ctx)`), converting the nesting into a flat
   sequence of guard clauses. **Reuse the existing `_emit` helper (`differ.py:134`)
   â€” do not create a new one.** Keep the 4-way semantic and the documented
   dd-4/dd-5 binding rules intact. Confirm CC drops (radon) and behavior is
   identical via `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_differ.py`.
2. **`_apply_inbound_update`:** build a declarative
   `FIELD_MAPPERS = {"priority": _resolve_priority, "assignee": _extract_name, â€¦}`
   for the simple scalar fields and loop over the present ones; keep **both** the
   `status` branch (second STATUS event + reverse-map) and the `labels` branch
   (tag read-modify-write) special-cased. Run the inbound apply suites
   (`tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/mutate/test_inbound_*`).
3. **`create_one`:** introduce a `DispatchContext` dataclass carrying
   `rest_budget`/`rest_calls`, `deferred_creates`, `events_list`, `comment_errors`,
   `binding_store`, `repo_root`, `client`; thread one `ctx` through `create_one`
   instead of the loose out-params, making the shared accumulator state explicit.
   Replace the magic `200` (`:95`/`:125`) with a named module constant
   `_REST_CALL_BUDGET = 200`. `update_one` is already a 3-param function â€” leave its
   signature (optionally accept `ctx` only for `comment_errors`). Run the
   orchestrate/dispatch suites (`tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/orchestrate/**`).
4. Re-run radon on the three functions to confirm complexity dropped, and run the
   full reconciler unit tier.

## Tradeoffs

- **Benefit:** lower CC + nesting; adding a synced field becomes a one-line table
  entry; the shared dispatch state is explicit instead of three loose mutable
  lists; the REST budget is a named, single-sourced constant.
- **Cost/risk:** these are behavior-preserving structural refactors in
  sync-critical code â€” every step must be backed by the existing (extensive)
  reconciler tests, which the audit confirmed are proportionate to the risk. Do NOT
  let the field-map table swallow the special `status` branch. Keep the dd-4/dd-5
  binding semantics in `compute_mutations` exactly.
- **Explicitly excluded:** `reconcile_once` â€” flattening its spine would obscure
  the fetchâ†’diffâ†’apply pass narrative for no complexity-of-understanding gain.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `compute_mutations`'s unbound-local cascade is flattened into guard-clause
  helpers (reusing the existing `_emit`); radon CC is materially lower;
  `test_differ.py` passes unchanged.
- [ ] `_apply_inbound_update` uses a field-map table for the simple scalar fields,
  with **both** the `status` and `labels` branches still special-cased; inbound
  apply tests pass.
- [ ] `create_one` takes a `DispatchContext` instead of loose mutable out-params;
  the REST budget is a named constant; `update_one`'s small signature is unchanged
  (or takes `ctx` only for `comment_errors`); dispatch tests pass.
- [ ] `reconcile_once` is untouched.
- [ ] ruff + mypy + the full reconciler unit tier stay green; no behavioral diff
  in a reconcile dry-run.
</content>
data.title: Reduce accidental complexity in the reconciler differ/applier
